Rest and Motion

Solution : The velocity of the food packet at the time of release is u and is horizontal. The vertical velocity at the time of release is zero.

I D 1 Figure 3-W7

Vertical motion : If ¢ be the time taken by the packet to reach the victim, we have for vertical motion,

12 (2H. | H=~ st or, t= 2 .. ()

Horizontal motion : If D be the horizontal distance travelled by the packet, we have D = ut. Putting ¢ from

(i), D= Ve

The distance between the victim and the packet at the time of release is

_ 2 Vor Hg

13. A particle is projected horizontally with a speed u from the top of a plane inclined at an angle 8 with the horizontal. How far from the point of projection will the particle strike the plane ?

Solution : Take X,Y-axes as shown in figure (3-W8). Suppose that the particle strikes the plane at a point P with coordinates (x, y). Consider the motion between A and P.

(We

Figure 3-W8 Motion in x-direction : Initial velocity = u Acceleration = 0 x =ut. .. (i) Motion in y-direction : Initial velocity = 0 Acceleration = g

y= Sat’. .. (i)

: Kinematics 45

Eliminating ¢ from (i) and (ii)

_l ix?) Y= 982 Also y =x tand. 2 2u “tand Thus, o> x tand giving x = 0, or, —« u 2u “tand

Clearly the point P corresponds to x =

2u* tan’ then y = x tand = ————— -

The distance AP =/ = Vex +y ° 2 = - tand V1+tan‘6

Qu”

tand seo.

Alternatively : Take the axes as shown in figure 3-W9. Consider the motion between A and P.

Figure 3-W9

Motion along the X-axis : Initial velocity = u cos0 Acceleration = g sin0

Displacement = AP. Thus, AP =(u cos 6) t + 5 (g sin@) t”. . @ Motion along the Y-axis :

Initial velocity = - u sind Acceleration = g cos0

Displacement = 0.

Thus, 0 =- ut sinO + 5 gt “cos 2u sind or, t= . g cos0 2u sinO Clearly, the point P corresponds to t = oa“. g cosO

Putting this value of ¢ in (i),

2 g sin /2u sind 2 g cos0

Ou si AP =(u cos6) t a

g cosO

Qu" sind 2u’ sind tan’ 0 = + 8 8

2 2 = - sinO sec 0 = 2u

tan® secO.
